response:
Sub-problem 1: The director has planned scenes inspired by 5 different iconic Akshay Kumar movies. Each scene requires a different number of shooting days, specifically: 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 days respectively. To optimize the schedule, the director wants to shoot these scenes in such a way that the total number of shooting days is minimized while ensuring that no two consecutive scenes come from movies released in the same decade. Represent the decades as sets: {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, and {2010-2019}. Given the release years of the movies as 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015, devise an optimal shooting schedule that minimizes the total number of shooting days subject to the stated constraint.

The director has five scenes with shooting days 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 days respectively. The release years of the movies are 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015. These years fall into the decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, and {2010-2019} respectively. The constraint is that no two consecutive scenes can come from movies released in the same decade. The goal is to minimize the total number of shooting days while satisfying this constraint.

An optimal schedule would be to alternate the scenes between the decades. The scenes from 1994 (1990-1999) and 2004 (2000-2009) are in different decades, so they can be shot in sequence. The scene from 2007 (2000-2009) must be separated from the 2004 scene. The scene from 2012 (2010-2019) must be separated from the 2015 scene. The optimal schedule is:

- Scene 1: 4 days (1994)  
- Scene 2: 6 days (2004)  
- Scene 3: 8 days (2007)  
-
